sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Kayla Ward, a 7th-grade student, presents with a 2-year history of acne on her face, which is present most every day.
She has been using Persa-Gel and washing regularly, which is somewhat helpful.
There are no associated symptoms including itching, bleeding, or pain.
No additional past medical history.
She lives with her parents and sister and has a dog, bird, and bunnies.
She wears sunscreen in the summer with SPF 30.
Past medical history: No additional past medical history.
Review of systems: No additional symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: Persa-Gel [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: Full exam is performed today, except for under the underwear and under the bra.
Multiple benign nevi on the trunk and extremities.
Scattered skin-colored papules.
Open and closed comedones.
Erythematous papules on the face, primarily on the forehead and with also some on the central cheeks and chin.
The chest and back are relatively spared.
The remainder of the examination is normal.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: Mild to moderate acne, mostly comedonal with small inflammatory component.
Problem list: Acne; Acne (status: active)
Plan:
I prescribed adapalene 0.1% cream to be applied in a thin layer to the areas she generally gets acne, starting with a few nights a week and slowly working up to every night.

She will also use a mild cleanser and a non-comedogenic moisturizer with sunscreen SPF 30 or higher in the morning, and wash her face with the same cleanser and apply a non-comedogenic moisturizer at night.
She will also use Clean and Clear Persa-Gel in a thin layer on the areas she generally gets acne.
I advised her to wear sunscreen when outside due to potential sensitivity and dryness or irritation from the adapalene.
Follow-up: I plan to see Kayla in six months to assess her progress.
